history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed at upgrade_spring_boot_3_3 because javax.mail.MessagingException is missing after the Spring Boot 3.x migration, which switches to Jakarta EE namespaces.
history.qwen_rationale: Spring Boot 3.x no longer provides javax.mail on the classpath; it uses jakarta.mail instead. Adding JavaxMigrationToJakarta to the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade step will automatically rewrite javax.mail imports to jakarta.mail, allowing the code to compile against the Jakarta Mail API provided by spring-boot-starter-mail.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation fails with 'cannot access javax.mail.MessagingException' after Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade because the Jakarta migration recipe skipped javax.mail imports, likely due to the target jakarta.mail classes being absent from the classpath during recipe execution.
history.qwen_rationale: Adding jakarta.mail dependencies via a pom_patch step before the Spring Boot upgrade ensures the jakarta namespace classes are available on the classpath. This allows JavaxMigrationToJakarta to correctly match and rewrite javax.mail imports to jakarta.mail before the step's compilation check, resolving the missing class error.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation fails with 'cannot access javax.mail.MessagingException' during Spring Boot 3 upgrade because the SB3 BOM removes javax.mail dependencies and the source code still references the old namespace.
history.qwen_rationale: Adding correct jakarta.mail dependencies (2.1.3) via pom_patch ensures the target classes are available on the classpath. Running JavaxMigrationToJakarta explicitly before the Spring Boot upgrade step guarantees imports are rewritten to jakarta.* before compilation validates against the new SB3 classpath, preventing the missing-class error.

history.qwen_observation: The explicit JavaxMigrationToJakarta step ran before the Spring Boot 3.3 upgrade, changing source imports to jakarta.* while the classpath still contained javax.* dependencies, causing compilation failures for missing jakarta packages.
history.qwen_rationale: Remove the explicit JavaxMigrationToJakarta and add_jakarta_mail_deps steps. Rely on UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 to handle the javax-to-jakarta migration and dependency updates internally, as it is designed to compose these changes safely when upgrading from SB 2.x to 3.x.

history.qwen_observation: Compilation failed at upgrade_spring_boot_3_3 because javax.mail.MessagingException could not be found, indicating missing jakarta.mail dependency and incomplete javax-to-jakarta migration.
history.qwen_rationale: Adding jakarta.mail and jakarta.persistence dependencies via pom_patch ensures they are on the classpath before the Spring Boot 3 upgrade. Explicitly including JavaxMigrationToJakarta alongside UpgradeSpringBoot_3_3 guarantees all javax imports are converted to jakarta, resolving the missing class error during the JDK 17 compilation step.
